name: 2026-mcp-reviewer-survey-methodology
description: MCP reviewer survey methodology and findings for VIGOR-1cbb: (1) zero public 2026 MCP servers wrap VideoScore2 — must wrap (~250-400 LOC, GPU, http/sse). (2) FreeCAD/OpenSCAD MCP space is mature; neka-nat/freecad-mcp + quellant/openscad-mcp cover the surface, but execute_code on neka-nat is shell-equivalent and MUST be denied at allowlist. (3) Photo VLM critique: use Claude Vision in-process (zero-MCP path), not a wrapper — VIGOR is already a Claude agent. (4) Mesh manifold/watertight has no MCP server; trivial in-adapter trimesh wrap (80-150 LOC). (5) AIECF has zero public MCP presence; integration is a wrap-cost problem, not a discovery problem.

name: ops-v1-host-audit-2026-05-method
description: When auditing OPS v1 host adoption, the canonical-spec-vs-shipping-host gap is the load-bearing finding: every host audited (Claude Code, Goose, Amp, Hermes, Strands, Gemini CLI, Junie, Cursor) uses a vendor-specific manifest path or no manifest at all; only Claude Code's .claude-plugin/plugin.json is structurally compatible (vendor-prefixed precedence per OPS spec sec. 5). Recommendation patterns that ship only the OPS-canonical path will not load anywhere today.

name: ops-v1-pr3-pending-breaking-change
description: vercel-labs/open-plugin-spec PR #3 (codex/gav-readme-governance-suggestions, opened 2026-05-04, unmerged as of 2026-05-14) proposes moving the canonical OPS manifest from .plugin/plugin.json to root-level plugin.json and adds a required id field with URL-style provenance. Pinning OPS adopters to a specific commit (cd5f34e7) is necessary until the path stabilizes. Re-check before any ADR cites OPS v1 normatively.
